title: Avoid copy of existing layers
body:
When copying an image from a Docker daemon to a local Docker registry, existing layer are copyied even if they already exists on the destination.
This is not the behavior of the Docker pull command.

Is this an expected behavior?

comments.body:
Ok, thanks.
So, it seems it is because layers are compressed on the fly, so we cannot know the sha before the upload. 
If it is true, do you know how docker does?

It keeps a local ~cache of compressed digest ↔︎ uncompressed digest correspondences; so, it can detect that the compressed equivalent is already stored on a registry, or that a decompressed layer is already available locally.
hmm... so there is a bug then :/ because this is not the behavior I'm observing.
There may well be. Provide exact steps to reproduce, and if it seems relevant, a `--debug` output, please.
(To be explicit, the ~cache is a Docker daemon behavior, not yet available in containers/image.)
@mtrmac ah ok, sorry, I didn't get your were talking about Docker. This would be a nice feature then:)
Dumb question but is there really no way of not copying when dest is already up to date? That makes Skopeo unusable for us.

@abergmeier I don't believe there is currently a way to copy and skip layers/images already locally stored with Skopeo.  But that does seem like a nice feature, something like `podman build --pull=false..`  where the image is pulled only if it isn't already present?  Perhaps a new `--copy-if-needed` option that could be used instead of `--copy`.  I'm going to reopen this and I'm sure @mtrmac or @vrothberg will have further insights/thoughts.

For CloudBuild I now solved this by going back to using docker:

```
steps:
  - id: Warm up local docker cache
    name: gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker
    entrypoint: bash
    args:
    - '-c'
    - |
      docker pull eu.gcr.io/test/maven:3.6.3-openjdk-14 || exit 0
  - id: Pull latest maven image
    name: gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker
    args:
    - pull
    - maven:3.6.3-openjdk-14
  - id: Set local maven image to latest state
    name: gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker
    args:
    - tag
    - maven:3.6.3-openjdk-14
    - eu.gcr.io/test/maven:3.6.3-openjdk-14
  - id: Populate cached maven image
    name: gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker
    args:
    - push
    - eu.gcr.io/test/maven:3.6.3-openjdk-14
```

and docker is smart enough to do the "minimum" amount of data transfering.

> Dumb question but is there really no way of not copying when dest is already up to date?

Typically, `skopeo copy` will recognize that layers are already present and not copy them, and always write only the tiny metadata. That should be basically indistinguishable from checking whether the destination is up to date and not copying.

> That makes Skopeo unusable for us.

Please file new issues instead of piling on two year old ones; if you are “going back to Docker” it’s not at all obvious that it is related to the original issue, especially when there _is_ now a blob info cache. And

> Provide exact steps to reproduce, and if it seems relevant, a --debug output, please.

Testing with a recent version of Skopeo:
```console
 sudo bin/skopeo copy docker-daemon:registry:2 --dest-tls-verify=false docker://localhost:5000/foo
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 9d08b7a37338 done  
Copying blob 588f0b714a86 done  
Copying blob c62467775792 done  
Copying blob a330d9dc14ce done  
Copying blob 7444ea29e45e done  
Copying config 708bc6af7e done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
[mitr@f28 skopeo]$ sudo bin/skopeo copy docker-daemon:registry:2 --dest-tls-verify=false docker://localhost:5000/foo
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob a330d9dc14ce skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 9d08b7a37338 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob c62467775792 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 588f0b714a86 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 7444ea29e45e [--------------------------------------] 0.0b / 0.0b
Copying config 708bc6af7e [======================================] 3.1KiB / 3.1KiB
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
```
does skip layers that are known to exist on a destination, using a blob cache.

If this does not work for you, please file a separate issue with exact steps to reproduce, and if it seems relevant, a `--debug` output.
